sdl2's fullscreen methods are not useful for me. what other ways can i set my program to be fullscreened in c++ sdl2

If the SDL2 fullscreen methods are not suitable for your needs, you can achieve fullscreen mode in SDL2 by utilizing SDL_DisplayMode and SDL_SetWindowFullscreen functions. Here's a step-by-step process to set your program to fullscreen mode using C++ and SDL2:

1. Include the necessary headers:
```cpp
#include <SDL2/SDL.h>
```

2. Initialize SDL:
```cpp
SDL_Init(SDL_INIT_VIDEO);
```

3. Create an SDL window in windowed mode:
```cpp
SDL_Window* window = SDL_CreateWindow("Fullscreen Example", SDL_WINDOWPOS_UNDEFINED, SDL_WINDOWPOS_UNDEFINED, 800, 600, SDL_WINDOW_OPENGL);
```

4. Get the current desktop's display mode:
```cpp
SDL_DisplayMode displayMode;
SDL_GetCurrentDisplayMode(0, &displayMode);
```
This will retrieve the current display mode of the primary monitor (index 0) and store it in the displayMode struct.

5. Set the window to fullscreen mode:
```cpp
SDL_SetWindowFullscreen(window, SDL_WINDOW_FULLSCREEN);
```
This will set the window to fullscreen mode on the primary monitor.

6. Set the window size to match the current display mode:
```cpp
SDL_SetWindowSize(window, displayMode.w, displayMode.h);
```
This step ensures that the window matches the screen resolution.

7. Render or perform tasks in your fullscreen SDL window as needed.

8. Cleanup and quit SDL:
```cpp
SDL_DestroyWindow(window);
SDL_Quit();
```

Keep in mind that the above code assumes that you are using a single SDL window and rendering context. If you have more complex requirements or multiple windows, you may need to adjust the code accordingly.

Remember to handle any errors that may arise during initialization and ensure proper error checking throughout your code.

Please note that when switching to fullscreen mode, SDL will handle the fullscreen display change, which may cause your existing rendering context to be invalidated. Make sure you reinitialize the graphics or rendering context after switching to fullscreen if needed.
cool. how can i do that but make the window borderless and set to {0, 0}?

To make the SDL window borderless and positioned at coordinates {0, 0}, you can modify the SDL_CreateWindow function with the SDL_WINDOW_BORDERLESS flag and provide the desired x and y coordinates for window creation. Here's an updated version of the code:

```cpp
SDL_Init(SDL_INIT_VIDEO);

SDL_Window* window = SDL_CreateWindow("Fullscreen Example", 0, 0, 800, 600, SDL_WINDOW_OPENGL | SDL_WINDOW_BORDERLESS);
```

By adding the SDL_WINDOW_BORDERLESS flag, you create a borderless window, and you can modify the x and y parameters (which are the window's initial position) to 0, which positions the window at coordinates {0, 0}.

Rest of the code remains the same, such as getting the current display mode, setting the window to fullscreen mode, and adjusting the window size to match the display mode.

Keep in mind that using a borderless window can affect mouse input and window management. Handle user input and window controls accordingly if using a borderless window.

Don't forget to clean up your resources and quit SDL when you're done:

```cpp
SDL_DestroyWindow(window);
SDL_Quit();
```

Remember to handle any errors that may occur during initialization and perform proper error checking throughout your code.
